title: Correlate k6 results with Grafana observability

blocks.content:
Welcome to the learning path that walks performance engineers, SREs, and solutions engineers from a Grafana Cloud k6 spike test through metrics, logs, and traces so you can judge whether a passing test really means the system is ready—especially during a trial or proof of value.

You use one UTC window in **Explore** to compare k6 results with synthetic probe metrics and logs, then correlate traces from the k6 test result when Grafana Cloud Traces is enabled. The lab uses a quickpizza-style demo target so you can finish in one sitting; the workflow applies to any service you already instrument.

The image below shows an example Grafana Cloud k6 test overview you use as the anchor before you open **Explore**.

![Grafana Cloud k6 test overview showing performance trends for a run, the starting point before you correlate with metrics, logs, and traces in Explore](/media/docs/k6/screenshot-grafana-cloud-k6-test-overview-trends.png)

## Here's what to expect

When you complete this path, you'll be able to:

- Verify Grafana Cloud k6, `grafanacloud-k6`, and your metrics, logs, and traces data sources before you run a spike, then align generator and application metrics on one time range in **Explore**
- Run a controlled spike test in Grafana Cloud k6 with a live metrics view, review the **Test Result** view for thresholds and checks, and capture a UTC anchor time plus baseline numbers for analysis
- Compare k6 latency with synthetic probe metrics in **Explore**
- Classify errors and timing from logs in Loki for the demo service (application or synthetic monitoring, depending on your stack)
- Use k6 **View Traces** (or optional **Explore** TraceQL) when your stack has Tempo data, or document a traces readiness gap
- Draft a short postmortem narrative that ties the signals together

## Before you begin

Before you correlate a Grafana Cloud k6 spike with metrics, logs, and traces, ensure you have:

- A Grafana Cloud account. To create one, refer to [Grafana Cloud](https://grafana.com/signup/cloud/connect-account/).
- Access to Grafana Cloud k6 and to **Explore** with the `grafanacloud-k6` data source plus metrics, logs, and traces data sources connected (Grafana Cloud often labels the metrics source Prometheus).
- Enough familiarity to paste and run a small k6 script. If you need a primer first, complete [Run your first k6 performance test](/docs/learning-paths/run-first-k6-test/).

This path works best when you load-test a service you instrument and observe, or when you record telemetry gaps in your postmortem (for example missing **View Traces** or only synthetic monitoring logs on a demo stack). It does not replace first-time k6 onboarding or end-to-end log or trace setup. For ingestion and the product k6–Tempo integration, refer to [Send logs to Grafana Cloud using Alloy](/docs/learning-paths/send-logs-alloy-loki/), [Send traces to Grafana Cloud using Alloy](/docs/learning-paths/send-traces-alloy/), and [Correlate results with Grafana Cloud Traces](/docs/grafana-cloud/testing/k6/analyze-results/integration-with-grafana-cloud-traces/).

## Troubleshooting

If you get stuck, we've got your back! Where appropriate, troubleshooting information is just a click away.

## More to explore

We understand you might want to explore other capabilities not strictly on this path. We'll provide you opportunities where it makes sense.
